parts.text:
Review the current uncommitted changes in the axe project at /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe. Run `git diff` and check for:

1. **Version bumps**: Verify "1.5.0" appears in cmd/root.go, internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go, and cmd/version_test.go — and that "1.4.0" no longer appears in those files.

2. **CHANGELOG.md**: Verify the new [1.5.0] section is properly formatted with correct Keep a Changelog structure, the date is 2026-03-20, and the reference link at the bottom is correct.

3. **README.md**: Check that:
   - The three new features appear in the Features list
   - The Run Flags table has --max-tokens and --agents-dir rows
   - The Exit Codes table exists with codes 0-4
   - The Agent Configuration example TOML includes `allowed_hosts` and `[budget]`
   - The Output Allowlist section exists with a behavior table
   - The Token Budget section exists with TOML and flag examples
   - The Local Agent Directories section exists with auto-discovery, override, resolution order, and smart scaffolding
   - No existing content was accidentally removed or corrupted
   - All markdown tables are properly formatted (pipes aligned, header separators present)
   - No broken markdown (unclosed code blocks, etc.)

Return any issues found, or confirm everything looks correct.
I'll load the auto-review skill and then examine the uncommitted changes systematically.

state.output:
<skill_content name="auto-review">
# Skill: auto-review

Your job is to provide a comprehesive review of current code changes.


## Rules

- Always use the Context7 MCP for library/API documentation, code generation, setup, or configuration steps.
- Check for the following:
    - bugs
    - security issues
    - code drift
    - performance bottlenecks
    - poor maintainability

## Steps

With a @general subagent:
1. Use `git diff HEAD` to get the changes.
2. Do a full code review on the diff:
    - Evaluate whether each change makes sense in the broader system.
    - Confirm the code does what it’s intended to do, especially for user-facing or critical paths.
    - Prioritize clear variable and function names, simple logic, and minimal complexity.
    - Ensure tests are meaningful, cover edge cases, and fail when the code breaks. Tests should be as maintainable as production code.
    - Avoid nitpicks that block progress unless they are critical. Use “nit” labels for minor style issues and mark non-blocking feedback clearly.
3. Your review output MUST be a markdown list and include:
    - A prompt for an LLM coding agent to fix the code. This prompt MUST be a specific and only address the fix needed.
    - Make sure to mention the file name prepend with `@` in the prompt.
4. Once the review is complete, return the review to the caller.

Base directory for this skill: file:///Users/jaronswab/.config/opencode/skills/auto-review
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
state.title: Loaded skill: auto-review

input.description: Show diff summary of uncommitted changes

parts.text: Good. Let me now get the full diff and read the affected files to do a thorough review.

input.description: Get full diff of all uncommitted changes

state.output:
diff --git a/CHANGELOG.md b/CHANGELOG.md
index 1af46e5..1a29f57 100644
--- a/CHANGELOG.md
+++ b/CHANGELOG.md
@@ -5,6 +5,17 @@ All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
 and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
 
+## [1.5.0] - 2026-03-20
+
+### Added
+- Output allowlist for outgoing connections (#45)
+- Local agent directory support (#41)
+- Token budget limits per agent run (#37)
+
+### Changed
+- Consolidate Docker release workflow (#44)
+- Bump github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk from 1.4.0 to 1.4.1 (#39)
+
 ## [1.4.0] - 2026-03-17
 
 ### Added
@@ -70,6 +81,7 @@ and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0
 - staticcheck/errcheck lint issues
 - Duplicate error silencing, glob validation, and nil ExitError guard
 
+[1.5.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.4.0...v1.5.0
 [1.4.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.3.0...v1.4.0
 [1.3.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.2.0...v1.3.0
 [1.2.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.1.1...v1.2.0
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 7268b6d..b5f6ac4 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -25,9 +25,12 @@ Axe is the executor, not the scheduler. It is designed to be composed with stand
 - **Memory garbage collection** — LLM-assisted pattern analysis and trimming
 - **Skill system** — reusable instruction sets that can be shared across agents
 - **Stdin piping** — pipe any output directly into an agent (`git diff | axe run reviewer`)
+- **Local agent directories** — auto-discovers agents from `<cwd>/axe/agents/` before the global config, or use `--agents-dir` to point anywhere
 - **Dry-run mode** — inspect resolved context without calling the LLM
 - **JSON output** — structured output with metadata for scripting
 - **Built-in tools** — file operations (read, write, edit, list) sandboxed to working directory; shell command execution; URL fetching; web search
+- **Output allowlist** — restrict `url_fetch` and `web_search` to specific hostnames; private/reserved IPs are always blocked (SSRF protection)
+- **Token budgets** — cap cumulative token usage per agent run via `[budget]` config or `--max-tokens` flag
 - **MCP tool support** — connect to external MCP servers for additional tools via SSE or streamable-HTTP transport
 - **Configurable retry** — exponential, linear, or fixed backoff for transient provider errors (429, 5xx, timeouts)
 - **Minimal dependencies** — four direct dependencies (cobra, toml, mcp-go-sdk, x/net); all LLM calls use the standard library
@@ -265,9 +268,21 @@ Config is read-write because `axe config init` and `axe agents init` write into
 | `--skill <path>` | from TOML | Override the skill file path |
 | `--workdir <path>` | from TOML or cwd | Override the working directory |
 | `--timeout <seconds>` | 120 | Request timeout |
+| `--max-tokens <int>` | 0 (unlimited) | Cap cumulative token usage for the run (exit code 4 if exceeded) |
 | `--dry-run` | false | Show resolved context without calling the LLM |
 | `--verbose` / `-v` | false | Print debug info (model, timing, tokens, retries) to stderr |
 | `--json` | false | Wrap output in a JSON envelope with metadata |
+| `--agents-dir <path>` | (auto-discover) | Override agent search directory |
+
+### Exit Codes
+
+| Code | Meaning |
+|---|---|
+| 0 | Success |
+| 1 | Runtime error |
+| 2 | Configuration error |
+| 3 | Provider/network error |
+| 4 | Token budget exceeded |
 
 ## Agent Configuration
 
@@ -283,6 +298,7 @@ files = ["src/**/*.go", "CONTRIBUTING.md"]
 workdir = "/home/user/projects/myapp"
 tools = ["read_file", "list_directory", "run_command"]
 sub_agents = ["test-runner", "lint-checker"]
+allowed_hosts = ["api.example.com", "docs.example.com"]
 
 [sub_agents_config]
 max_depth = 3       # maximum nesting depth (hard max: 5)
@@ -304,6 +320,9 @@ headers = { Authorization = "Bearer ${MY_TOKEN}" }
 temperature = 0.3
 max_tokens = 4096
 
+[budget]
+max_tokens = 50000    # 0 = unlimited (default)
+
 [retry]
 max_retries = 3           # retry up to 3 times on transient errors
 backoff = "exponential"   # "exponential", "linear", or "fixed"
@@ -336,6 +355,43 @@ requests (400) are never retried. When `--verbose` is enabled, each retry
 attempt is logged to stderr. The `--json` envelope includes a `retry_attempts`
 field for observability.
 
+### Output Allowlist
+
+Agents that use `url_fetch` or `web_search` can be restricted to specific hostnames with the `allowed_hosts` field:
+
+```toml
+allowed_hosts = ["api.example.com", "docs.example.com"]
+```
+
+| Behavior | Detail |
+|---|---|
+| Empty or absent | All public hostnames allowed |
+| Non-empty list | Only exact hostname matches permitted (case-insensitive, no wildcard subdomains) |
+| Private IPs | Always blocked regardless of allowlist — loopback, link-local, RFC 1918, CGNAT, IPv6 private |
+| Redirects | Each redirect destination is re-validated against the allowlist and private IP check |
+| Sub-agents | Inherit the parent's `allowed_hosts` unless the sub-agent TOML explicitly sets its own |
+
+### Token Budget
+
+Cap cumulative token usage (input + output, across all turns and sub-agent calls) for a single run:
+
+```toml
+[budget]
+max_tokens = 50000   # 0 = unlimited (default)
+```
+
+Or override via flag:
+
+```bash
+axe run my-agent --max-tokens 10000
+```
+
+The flag takes precedence over TOML when set to a value greater than zero.
+
+When the budget is exceeded, the current response is returned but no further tool calls execute. The process exits with **code 4**. Memory is not appended on a budget-exceeded run.
+
+With `--verbose`, each turn logs cumulative usage to stderr. With `--json`, the output envelope includes `budget_max_tokens`, `budget_used_tokens`, and `budget_exceeded` fields (omitted when unlimited).
+
 ## Tools
 
 Agents can use built-in tools to interact with the filesystem and run commands. When tools are enabled, the agent enters a conversation loop — the LLM can make tool calls, receive results, and continue reasoning for up to 50 turns.
@@ -434,6 +490,43 @@ $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/axe/
             └── fetch.sh
 ```
 
+## Local Agent Directories
+
+By default, agents are loaded from `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/axe/agents/`. Axe also supports project-local agent directories for per-repo agent definitions.
+
+### Auto-Discovery
+
+If `<cwd>/axe/agents/` exists, axe searches it before the global config directory. A local agent with the same name as a global agent shadows the global one.
+
+```
+my-project/
+└── axe/
+    └── agents/
+        └── my-agent.toml   ← found automatically
+```
+
+### Explicit Override
+
+Use `--agents-dir` to point to any directory:
+
+```bash
+axe run my-agent --agents-dir ./custom/agents
+```
+
+This flag is available on all commands: `run`, `agents list`, `agents show`, `agents init`, `agents edit`, and `gc`.
+
+### Resolution Order
+
+1. `--agents-dir` (if provided)
+2. `<cwd>/axe/agents/` (auto-discovered)
+3. `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/axe/agents/` (global fallback)
+
+The first directory containing a matching `<name>.toml` wins.
+
+### Smart Scaffolding
+
+`axe agents init <name>` writes to `<cwd>/axe/agents/` if that directory already exists, otherwise falls back to the global config directory.
+
 ## Providers
 
 | Provider | API Key Env Var | Default Base URL |
diff --git a/cmd/root.go b/cmd/root.go
index afccdad..907f254 100644
--- a/cmd/root.go
+++ b/cmd/root.go
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ import (
 )
 
 // Version is the hardcoded version constant for the CLI.
-const Version = "1.4.0"
+const Version = "1.5.0"
 
 var rootCmd = &cobra.Command{
 	Use:   "axe",
diff --git a/cmd/version_test.go b/cmd/version_test.go
index fba2f90..c328ecb 100644
--- a/cmd/version_test.go
+++ b/cmd/version_test.go
@@ -16,14 +16,14 @@ func TestVersionCommand_Output(t *testing.T) {
 	}
 
 	got := buf.String()
-	want := "axe version 1.4.0\n"
+	want := "axe version 1.5.0\n"
 	if got != want {
 		t.Errorf("version output = %q, want %q", got, want)
 	}
 }
 
 func TestVersionConstant(t *testing.T) {
-	if Version != "1.4.0" {
-		t.Errorf("Version = %q, want %q", Version, "1.4.0")
+	if Version != "1.5.0" {
+		t.Errorf("Version = %q, want %q", Version, "1.5.0")
 	}
 }
diff --git a/internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go b/internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go
index f4b506f..12c515a 100644
--- a/internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go
+++ b/internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go
@@ -40,7 +40,7 @@ func Connect(ctx context.Context, cfg agent.MCPServerConfig) (*Client, error) {
 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported MCP transport %q", cfg.Transport)
 	}
 
-	client := mcp.NewClient(&mcp.Implementation{Name: "axe", Version: "1.4.0"}, nil)
+	client := mcp.NewClient(&mcp.Implementation{Name: "axe", Version: "1.5.0"}, nil)
 	session, err := client.Connect(ctx, transport, nil)
 	if err != nil {
 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("connect MCP server %q: %w", cfg.Name, err)

input.command: git diff HEAD -- cmd/root.go cmd/version_test.go internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go

state.title: Get version-related file diffs

parts.text: Now let me read the full CHANGELOG.md and relevant sections of README.md to verify nothing was corrupted:

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/CHANGELOG.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Changelog
2: 
3: All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
4: 
5: The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
6: and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
7: 
8: ## [1.5.0] - 2026-03-20
9: 
10: ### Added
11: - Output allowlist for outgoing connections (#45)
12: - Local agent directory support (#41)
13: - Token budget limits per agent run (#37)
14: 
15: ### Changed
16: - Consolidate Docker release workflow (#44)
17: - Bump github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk from 1.4.0 to 1.4.1 (#39)
18: 
19: ## [1.4.0] - 2026-03-17
20: 
21: ### Added
22: - AWS Bedrock provider support (#18)
23: - Retry logic for intermittent LLM provider failures (#35)
24: 
25: ### Fixed
26: - Add path sandbox enforcement to `run_command` tool (#31)
27: - Update Dockerfile to Go 1.25 and bump CI actions to Node 24
28: 
29: ## [1.3.0] - 2026-03-15
30: 
31: ### Added
32: - Google Gemini provider support
33: - MiniMax provider support (#27)
34: - GHCR docker publish workflow on version tags (#33)
35: 
36: ### Fixed
37: - Use max_completion_tokens for OpenAI provider (#34)
38: 
39: ## [1.2.0] - 2026-03-10
40: 
41: ### Added
42: - OpenCode provider support
43: - HTTP request timeout (15s per-request) for `url_fetch` tool
44: - HTML tag stripping for `url_fetch` responses
45: 
46: ## [1.1.1] - 2026-03-06
47: 
48: ### Fixed
49: - Remove hardcoded `/v1` from OpenAI provider URL path
50: 
51: ## [1.1.0] - 2026-03-06
52: 
53: ### Added
54: - MCP tool support
55: - `web_search` built-in tool using Tavily Search API
56: - `url_fetch` built-in tool
57: - JSON `tool_call_details` in run output
58: - Refusal detection in JSON output
59: - Executable examples
60: 
61: ### Changed
62: - Docker docs for running a single agent with selective mounts
63: 
64: ## [1.0.0] - 2026-03-02
65: 
66: ### Added
67: - Core CLI with `run`, `agents list`, `agents show`, `agents init`, `agents edit`, `gc`, and `config init` commands
68: - TOML-based agent configuration with SKILL.md context support
69: - Multi-provider LLM support: Anthropic, OpenAI, and Ollama
70: - Tool-calling system with `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `list_directory`, and `run_command` tools
71: - Sub-agent orchestration with `call_agent` tool and depth-limited execution
72: - Agent memory system with XDG-compliant data storage and garbage collection
73: - Path security: `~` and `$VAR` expansion for user-supplied paths
74: - Stdin piping support for composing with Unix tools
75: - Dry-run mode and per-tool verbose logging
76: - Docker containerization support
77: - CI workflows: lint, test, build, and GoReleaser
78: 
79: ### Fixed
80: - Arg validation UX and skill path resolution
81: - staticcheck/errcheck lint issues
82: - Duplicate error silencing, glob validation, and nil ExitError guard
83: 
84: [1.5.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.4.0...v1.5.0
85: [1.4.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.3.0...v1.4.0
86: [1.3.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.2.0...v1.3.0
87: [1.2.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.1.1...v1.2.0
88: [1.1.1]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.1.0...v1.1.1
89: [1.1.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.0.0...v1.1.0
90: [1.0.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/releases/tag/v1.0.0

(End of file - total 90 lines)
</content>
state.title: CHANGELOG.md

metadata.preview:
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [1.5.0] - 2026-03-20

### Added
- Output allowlist for outgoing connections (#45)
- Local agent directory support (#41)
- Token budget limits per agent run (#37)

### Changed
- Consolidate Docker release workflow (#44)
- Bump github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk from 1.4.0 to 1.4.1 (#39)

## [1.4.0] - 2026-03-17

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Axe
2: 
3: ![axe banner](banner.png)
4: 
5: A CLI tool for managing and running LLM-powered agents.
6: 
7: ## Why Axe?
8: 
9: Most AI tooling assumes you want a chatbot. A long-running session with a massive context window doing everything at once. But that's not how good software works. Good software is small, focused, and composable.
10: 
11: Axe treats LLM agents the same way Unix treats programs. Each agent does one thing well. You define it in a TOML file, give it a focused skill, and run it from the command line. Pipe data in, get results out. Chain agents together. Trigger them from cron, git hooks, or CI. Whatever you already use. No daemon, no GUI, no framework to buy into. Just a binary and your configs.
12: 
13: ## Overview
14: 
15: Axe orchestrates LLM-powered agents defined via TOML configuration files. Each agent has its own system prompt, model selection, skill files, context files, working directory, persistent memory, and the ability to delegate to sub-agents.
16: 
17: Axe is the executor, not the scheduler. It is designed to be composed with standard Unix tools — cron, git hooks, pipes, file watchers — rather than reinventing scheduling or workflow orchestration.
18: 
19: ## Features
20: 
21: - **Multi-provider support** — Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama (local models), OpenCode, and AWS Bedrock
22: - **TOML-based agent configuration** — declarative, version-controllable agent definitions
23: - **Sub-agent delegation** — agents can call other agents via LLM tool use, with depth limiting and parallel execution
24: - **Persistent memory** — timestamped markdown logs that carry context across runs
25: - **Memory garbage collection** — LLM-assisted pattern analysis and trimming
26: - **Skill system** — reusable instruction sets that can be shared across agents
27: - **Stdin piping** — pipe any output directly into an agent (`git diff | axe run reviewer`)
28: - **Local agent directories** — auto-discovers agents from `<cwd>/axe/agents/` before the global config, or use `--agents-dir` to point anywhere
29: - **Dry-run mode** — inspect resolved context without calling the LLM
30: - **JSON output** — structured output with metadata for scripting
31: - **Built-in tools** — file operations (read, write, edit, list) sandboxed to working directory; shell command execution; URL fetching; web search
32: - **Output allowlist** — restrict `url_fetch` and `web_search` to specific hostnames; private/reserved IPs are always blocked (SSRF protection)
33: - **Token budgets** — cap cumulative token usage per agent run via `[budget]` config or `--max-tokens` flag
34: - **MCP tool support** — connect to external MCP servers for additional tools via SSE or streamable-HTTP transport
35: - **Configurable retry** — exponential, linear, or fixed backoff for transient provider errors (429, 5xx, timeouts)
36: - **Minimal dependencies** — four direct dependencies (cobra, toml, mcp-go-sdk, x/net); all LLM calls use the standard library
37: 
38: ## Installation
39: 
40: Requires Go 1.25+.
41: 
42: ```bash
43: go install github.com/jrswab/axe@latest
44: ```
45: 
46: Or build from source:
47: 
48: ```bash
49: git clone https://github.com/jrswab/axe.git
50: cd axe
51: go build .
52: ```
53: 
54: ## Quick Start
55: 
56: Initialize the configuration directory:
57: 
58: ```bash
59: axe config init
60: ```
61: 
62: This creates the directory structure at `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/axe/` with a sample skill and a default `config.toml` for provider credentials.
63: 
64: Scaffold a new agent:
65: 
66: ```bash
67: axe agents init my-agent
68: ```
69: 
70: Edit its configuration:
71: 
72: ```bash
73: axe agents edit my-agent
74: ```
75: 
76: Run the agent:
77: 
78: ```bash
79: axe run my-agent
80: ```
81: 
82: Pipe input from other tools:
83: 
84: ```bash
85: git diff --cached | axe run pr-reviewer
86: cat error.log | axe run log-analyzer
87: ```
88: 
89: ## Examples
90: 
91: The [`examples/`](examples/) directory contains ready-to-run agents you can copy into your config and use immediately. Includes a code reviewer, commit message generator, and text summarizer — each with a focused SKILL.md.
92: 
93: ```bash
94: # Copy an example agent into your config
95: cp examples/code-reviewer/code-reviewer.toml "$(axe config path)/agents/"
96: cp -r examples/code-reviewer/skills/ "$(axe config path)/skills/"
97: 
98: # Set your API key and run
99: export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="your-key-here"
100: git diff | axe run code-reviewer
101: ```
102: 
103: See [`examples/README.md`](examples/README.md) for full setup instructions.
104: 
105: ## Docker
106: 
107: Axe provides a Docker image for running agents in an isolated, hardened container.
108: 
109: ### Build the Image
110: 
111: ```bash
112: docker build -t axe .
113: ```
114: 
115: Multi-architecture builds (linux/amd64, linux/arm64) are supported via buildx:
116: 
117: ```bash
118: docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 -t axe:latest .
119: ```
120: 
121: ### Run an Agent
122: 
123: Mount your config directory and pass API keys as environment variables:
124: 
125: ```bash
126: docker run --rm \
127:   -v ./my-config:/home/axe/.config/axe \
128:   -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY \
129:   axe run my-agent
130: ```
131: 
132: Pipe stdin with the `-i` flag:
133: 
134: ```bash
135: git diff | docker run --rm -i \
136:   -v ./my-config:/home/axe/.config/axe \
137:   -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY \
138:   axe run pr-reviewer
139: ```
140: 
141: Without a config volume mounted, axe exits with code 2 (config error) because no agent TOML files exist.
142: 
143: ### Running a Single Agent
144: 
145: The examples above mount the entire config directory. If you only need to run one agent with one skill, mount just those files to their expected XDG paths inside the container. No `config.toml` is needed when API keys are passed via environment variables.
146: 
147: ```bash
148: docker run --rm -i \
149:   -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY \
150:   -v ./agents/reviewer.toml:/home/axe/.config/axe/agents/reviewer.toml:ro \
151:   -v ./skills/code-review/:/home/axe/.config/axe/skills/code-review/:ro \
152:   axe run reviewer
153: ```
154: 
155: The agent's `skill` field resolves automatically against the XDG config path inside the container, so no `--skill` flag is needed.
156: 
157: To use a **different skill** than the one declared in the agent's TOML, use the `--skill` flag to override it. In this case you only mount the replacement skill — the original skill declared in the TOML is ignored entirely:
158: 
159: ```bash
160: docker run --rm -i \
161:   -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY \
162:   -v ./agents/reviewer.toml:/home/axe/.config/axe/agents/reviewer.toml:ro \
163:   -v ./alt-review.md:/home/axe/alt-review.md:ro \
164:   axe run reviewer --skill /home/axe/alt-review.md
165: ```
166: 
167: If the agent declares `sub_agents`, all referenced agent TOMLs and their skills must also be mounted.
168: 
169: ### Persistent Data
170: 
171: Agent memory persists across runs when you mount a data volume:
172: 
173: ```bash
174: docker run --rm \
175:   -v ./my-config:/home/axe/.config/axe \
176:   -v axe-data:/home/axe/.local/share/axe \
177:   -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY \
178:   axe run my-agent
179: ```
180: 
181: ### Docker Compose
182: 
183: A `docker-compose.yml` is included for running axe alongside a local Ollama instance.
184: 
185: **Cloud provider only (no Ollama):**
186: 
187: ```bash
188: docker compose run --rm axe run my-agent
189: ```
190: 
191: **With Ollama sidecar:**
192: 
193: ```bash
194: docker compose --profile ollama up -d ollama
195: docker compose --profile cli run --rm axe run my-agent
196: ```
197: 
198: **Pull an Ollama model:**
199: 
200: ```bash
201: docker compose --profile ollama exec ollama ollama pull llama3
202: ```
203: 
204: > **Note:** The compose `axe` service declares `depends_on: ollama`. Docker Compose will attempt to start the Ollama service whenever axe is started via compose, even for cloud-only runs. For cloud-only usage without Ollama, use `docker run` directly instead of `docker compose run`.
205: 
206: ### Ollama on the Host
207: 
208: If Ollama runs directly on the host (not via compose), point to it with:
209: 
210: - **Linux:** `--add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway -e AXE_OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434`
211: - **macOS / Windows (Docker Desktop):** `-e AXE_OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434`
212: 
213: ### Security
214: 
215: The container runs with the following hardening by default (via compose):
216: 
217: - **Non-root user** — UID 10001
218: - **Read-only root filesystem** — writable locations are the config mount, data mount, and `/tmp/axe` tmpfs
219: - **All capabilities dropped** — `cap_drop: ALL`
220: - **No privilege escalation** — `no-new-privileges:true`
221: 
222: These settings do not restrict outbound network access. To isolate an agent that only talks to a local Ollama instance, add `--network=none` and connect it to the shared Docker network manually.
223: 
224: ### Volume Mounts
225: 
226: | Container Path | Purpose | Default Access |
227: |---|---|---|
228: | `/home/axe/.config/axe/` | Agent TOML files, skills, `config.toml` | Read-write |
229: | `/home/axe/.local/share/axe/` | Persistent memory files | Read-write |
230: 
231: Config is read-write because `axe config init` and `axe agents init` write into it. Mount as `:ro` if you only run agents.
232: 
233: ### Environment Variables
234: 
235: | Variable | Required | Purpose |
236: |---|---|---|
237: | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | If using Anthropic | API authentication |
238: | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | If using OpenAI | API authentication |
239: | `AXE_OLLAMA_BASE_URL` | If using Ollama | Ollama endpoint (default in compose: `http://ollama:11434`) |
240: | `AXE_ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` | No | Override Anthropic API endpoint |
241: | `AXE_OPENAI_BASE_URL` | No | Override OpenAI API endpoint |
242: | `AXE_OPENCODE_BASE_URL` | No | Override OpenCode API endpoint |
243: | `TAVILY_API_KEY` | If using web_search | Tavily web search API key |
244: | `AXE_WEB_SEARCH_BASE_URL` | No | Override web search endpoint |
245: 
246: ## CLI Reference
247: 
248: ### Commands
249: 
250: | Command | Description |
251: |---|---|
252: | `axe run <agent>` | Run an agent |
253: | `axe agents list` | List all configured agents |
254: | `axe agents show <agent>` | Display an agent's full configuration |
255: | `axe agents init <agent>` | Scaffold a new agent TOML file |
256: | `axe agents edit <agent>` | Open an agent TOML in `$EDITOR` |
257: | `axe config path` | Print the configuration directory path |
258: | `axe config init` | Initialize the config directory with defaults |
259: | `axe gc <agent>` | Run memory garbage collection for an agent |
260: | `axe gc --all` | Run GC on all memory-enabled agents |
261: | `axe version` | Print the current version |
262: 
263: ### Run Flags
264: 
265: | Flag | Default | Description |
266: |---|---|---|
267: | `--model <provider/model>` | from TOML | Override the model (e.g. `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514`) |
268: | `--skill <path>` | from TOML | Override the skill file path |
269: | `--workdir <path>` | from TOML or cwd | Override the working directory |
270: | `--timeout <seconds>` | 120 | Request timeout |
271: | `--max-tokens <int>` | 0 (unlimited) | Cap cumulative token usage for the run (exit code 4 if exceeded) |
272: | `--dry-run` | false | Show resolved context without calling the LLM |
273: | `--verbose` / `-v` | false | Print debug info (model, timing, tokens, retries) to stderr |
274: | `--json` | false | Wrap output in a JSON envelope with metadata |
275: | `--agents-dir <path>` | (auto-discover) | Override agent search directory |
276: 
277: ### Exit Codes
278: 
279: | Code | Meaning |
280: |---|---|
281: | 0 | Success |
282: | 1 | Runtime error |
283: | 2 | Configuration error |
284: | 3 | Provider/network error |
285: | 4 | Token budget exceeded |
286: 
287: ## Agent Configuration
288: 
289: Agents are defined as TOML files in `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/axe/agents/`.
290: 
291: ```toml
292: name = "pr-reviewer"
293: description = "Reviews pull requests for issues and improvements"
294: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
295: system_prompt = "You are a senior code reviewer. Be concise and actionable."
296: skill = "skills/code-review/SKILL.md"
297: files = ["src/**/*.go", "CONTRIBUTING.md"]
298: workdir = "/home/user/projects/myapp"
299: tools = ["read_file", "list_directory", "run_command"]
300: sub_agents = ["test-runner", "lint-checker"]
301: allowed_hosts = ["api.example.com", "docs.example.com"]
302: 
303: [sub_agents_config]
304: max_depth = 3       # maximum nesting depth (hard max: 5)
305: parallel = true     # run sub-agents concurrently
306: timeout = 120       # per sub-agent timeout in seconds
307: 
308: [memory]
309: enabled = true
310: last_n = 10         # load last N entries into context
311: max_entries = 100   # warn when exceeded
312: 
313: [[mcp_servers]]
314: name = "my-tools"
315: url = "https://my-mcp-server.example.com/sse"
316: transport = "sse"
317: headers = { Authorization = "Bearer ${MY_TOKEN}" }
318: 
319: [params]
320: temperature = 0.3
321: max_tokens = 4096
322: 
323: [budget]
324: max_tokens = 50000    # 0 = unlimited (default)
325: 
326: [retry]
327: max_retries = 3           # retry up to 3 times on transient errors
328: backoff = "exponential"   # "exponential", "linear", or "fixed"
329: initial_delay_ms = 500    # base delay before first retry
330: max_delay_ms = 30000      # maximum delay cap
331: 
332: [[mcp_servers]]
333: name = "filesystem"
334: transport = "stdio"
335: command = "/usr/local/bin/mcp-server-filesystem"
336: args = ["--root", "/home/user/projects"]
337: ```
338: 
339: All fields except `name` and `model` are optional.
340: 
341: ### Retry
342: 
343: Agents can retry on transient LLM provider errors — rate limits (429), server
344: errors (5xx), and timeouts. Retry is opt-in and disabled by default.
345: 
346: | Field | Default | Description |
347: |---|---|---|
348: | `max_retries` | 0 | Number of retry attempts after the initial request. 0 disables retry. |
349: | `backoff` | `"exponential"` | Strategy: `"exponential"` (with jitter), `"linear"`, or `"fixed"` |
350: | `initial_delay_ms` | 500 | Base delay in milliseconds before the first retry |
351: | `max_delay_ms` | 30000 | Maximum delay cap in milliseconds |
352: 
353: Only transient errors are retried. Authentication errors (401/403) and bad
354: requests (400) are never retried. When `--verbose` is enabled, each retry
355: attempt is logged to stderr. The `--json` envelope includes a `retry_attempts`
356: field for observability.
357: 
358: ### Output Allowlist
359: 
360: Agents that use `url_fetch` or `web_search` can be restricted to specific hostnames with the `allowed_hosts` field:
361: 
362: ```toml
363: allowed_hosts = ["api.example.com", "docs.example.com"]
364: ```
365: 
366: | Behavior | Detail |
367: |---|---|
368: | Empty or absent | All public hostnames allowed |
369: | Non-empty list | Only exact hostname matches permitted (case-insensitive, no wildcard subdomains) |
370: | Private IPs | Always blocked regardless of allowlist — loopback, link-local, RFC 1918, CGNAT, IPv6 private |
371: | Redirects | Each redirect destination is re-validated against the allowlist and private IP check |
372: | Sub-agents | Inherit the parent's `allowed_hosts` unless the sub-agent TOML explicitly sets its own |
373: 
374: ### Token Budget
375: 
376: Cap cumulative token usage (input + output, across all turns and sub-agent calls) for a single run:
377: 
378: ```toml
379: [budget]
380: max_tokens = 50000   # 0 = unlimited (default)
381: ```
382: 
383: Or override via flag:
384: 
385: ```bash
386: axe run my-agent --max-tokens 10000
387: ```
388: 
389: The flag takes precedence over TOML when set to a value greater than zero.
390: 
391: When the budget is exceeded, the current response is returned but no further tool calls execute. The process exits with **code 4**. Memory is not appended on a budget-exceeded run.
392: 
393: With `--verbose`, each turn logs cumulative usage to stderr. With `--json`, the output envelope includes `budget_max_tokens`, `budget_used_tokens`, and `budget_exceeded` fields (omitted when unlimited).
394: 
395: ## Tools
396: 
397: Agents can use built-in tools to interact with the filesystem and run commands. When tools are enabled, the agent enters a conversation loop — the LLM can make tool calls, receive results, and continue reasoning for up to 50 turns.
398: 
399: ### Built-in Tools
400: 
401: | Tool | Description |
402: |---|---|
403: | `list_directory` | List contents of a directory relative to the working directory |
404: | `read_file` | Read file contents with line-numbered output and optional pagination (offset/limit) |
405: | `write_file` | Create or overwrite a file, creating parent directories as needed |
406: | `edit_file` | Find and replace exact text in a file, with optional replace-all mode |
407: | `run_command` | Execute a shell command via `sh -c` and return combined output |
408: | `url_fetch` | Fetch URL content with HTML stripping and truncation |
409: | `web_search` | Search the web and return results |
410: | `call_agent` | Delegate a task to a sub-agent (controlled via `sub_agents`, not `tools`) |
411: 
412: Enable tools by adding them to the agent's `tools` field:
413: 
414: ```toml
415: tools = ["read_file", "list_directory", "run_command"]
416: ```
417: 
418: The `call_agent` tool is not listed in `tools` — it is automatically available when `sub_agents` is configured and the depth limit has not been reached.
419: 
420: ### Path Security
421: 
422: All file tools (`list_directory`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`) are sandboxed to the agent's working directory. Absolute paths, `..` traversal, and symlink escapes are rejected.
423: 
424: ### Parallel Execution
425: 
426: When an LLM returns multiple tool calls in a single turn, they run concurrently by default. This applies to both built-in tools and sub-agent calls. Disable with `parallel = false` in `[sub_agents_config]`.
427: 
428: ### MCP Tools
429: 
430: Agents can use tools from external [MCP](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/)
431: servers. Declare servers in the agent TOML with `[[mcp_servers]]`:
432: 
433: ```toml
434: [[mcp_servers]]
435: name = "my-tools"
436: url = "https://my-mcp-server.example.com/sse"
437: transport = "sse"
438: headers = { Authorization = "Bearer ${MY_TOKEN}" }
439: ```
440: 
441: At startup, axe connects to each declared server, discovers available tools via
442: `tools/list`, and makes them available to the LLM alongside built-in tools.
443: 
444: | Field | Required | Description |
445: |---|---|---|
446: | `name` | Yes | Human-readable identifier for the server |
447: | `url` | Yes | MCP server endpoint URL |
448: | `transport` | Yes | `"sse"` or `"streamable-http"` |
449: | `headers` | No | HTTP headers; values support `${ENV_VAR}` interpolation |
450: 
451: MCP tools are controlled entirely by `[[mcp_servers]]` — they are not listed in
452: the `tools` field. If an MCP tool has the same name as an enabled built-in tool,
453: the built-in takes precedence.
454: 
455: ## Skills
456: 
457: Skills are reusable instruction sets that provide an agent with domain-specific knowledge and workflows. They are defined as `SKILL.md` files following the community SKILL.md format.
458: 
459: ### Skill Resolution
460: 
461: The `skill` field in an agent TOML is resolved in order:
462: 
463: 1. **Absolute path** — used as-is (e.g. `/home/user/skills/SKILL.md`)
464: 2. **Relative to config dir** — e.g. `skills/code-review/SKILL.md` resolves to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/axe/skills/code-review/SKILL.md`
465: 3. **Bare name** — e.g. `code-review` resolves to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/axe/skills/code-review/SKILL.md`
466: 
467: ### Script Paths
468: 
469: Skills often reference helper scripts. Since `run_command` executes in the agent's `workdir` (not the skill directory), **script paths in SKILL.md must be absolute**. Relative paths will fail because the scripts don't exist in the agent's working directory.
470: 
471: ```
472: # Correct — absolute path
473: /home/user/.config/axe/skills/my-skill/scripts/fetch.sh <args>
474: 
475: # Wrong — relative path won't resolve from the agent's workdir
476: scripts/fetch.sh <args>
477: ```
478: 
479: ### Directory Structure
480: 
481: ```
482: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/axe/
483: ├── config.toml
484: ├── agents/
485: │   └── my-agent.toml
486: └── skills/
487:     └── my-skill/
488:         ├── SKILL.md
489:         └── scripts/
490:             └── fetch.sh
491: ```
492: 
493: ## Local Agent Directories
494: 
495: By default, agents are loaded from `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/axe/agents/`. Axe also supports project-local agent directories for per-repo agent definitions.
496: 
497: ### Auto-Discovery
498: 
499: If `<cwd>/axe/agents/` exists, axe searches it before the global config directory. A local agent with the same name as a global agent shadows the global one.
500: 
501: ```
502: my-project/
503: └── axe/
504:     └── agents/
505:         └── my-agent.toml   ← found automatically
506: ```
507: 
508: ### Explicit Override
509: 
510: Use `--agents-dir` to point to any directory:
511: 
512: ```bash
513: axe run my-agent --agents-dir ./custom/agents
514: ```
515: 
516: This flag is available on all commands: `run`, `agents list`, `agents show`, `agents init`, `agents edit`, and `gc`.
517: 
518: ### Resolution Order
519: 
520: 1. `--agents-dir` (if provided)
521: 2. `<cwd>/axe/agents/` (auto-discovered)
522: 3. `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/axe/agents/` (global fallback)
523: 
524: The first directory containing a matching `<name>.toml` wins.
525: 
526: ### Smart Scaffolding
527: 
528: `axe agents init <name>` writes to `<cwd>/axe/agents/` if that directory already exists, otherwise falls back to the global config directory.
529: 
530: ## Providers
531: 
532: | Provider | API Key Env Var | Default Base URL |
533: |---|---|---|
534: | Anthropic | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | `https://api.anthropic.com` |
535: | OpenAI | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | `https://api.openai.com` |
536: | Ollama | (none required) | `http://localhost:11434` |
537: | OpenCode | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` | Configurable |
538: | AWS Bedrock | (uses AWS credentials) | Region-based |
539: 
540: **AWS Bedrock Configuration:**
541: - Region: Set via `AWS_REGION` environment variable or `[providers.bedrock] region = "us-east-1"` in config.toml
542: - Credentials: Uses environment variables (`AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `AWS_SESSION_TOKEN`) or `~/.aws/credentials` file (supports `AWS_PROFILE` and `AWS_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE`)
543: - Model IDs: Use full Bedrock model IDs (e.g., `bedrock/anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0`)
544: 
545: Base URLs can be overridden with `AXE_<PROVIDER>_BASE_URL` environment variables or in `config.toml`.
546: 
547: ## License
548: 
549: Apache-2.0. See [LICENSE](LICENSE).

(End of file - total 549 lines)
</content>
state.title: README.md

metadata.preview:
# Axe

![axe banner](banner.png)

A CLI tool for managing and running LLM-powered agents.

## Why Axe?

Most AI tooling assumes you want a chatbot. A long-running session with a massive context window doing everything at once. But that's not how good software works. Good software is small, focused, and composable.

Axe treats LLM agents the same way Unix treats programs. Each agent does one thing well. You define it in a TOML file, give it a focused skill, and run it from the command line. Pipe data in, get results out. Chain agents together. Trigger them from cron, git hooks, or CI. Whatever you already use. No daemon, no GUI, no framework to buy into. Just a binary and your configs.

## Overview

Axe orchestrates LLM-powered agents defined via TOML configuration files. Each agent has its own system prompt, model selection, skill files, context files, working directory, persistent memory, and the ability to delegate to sub-agents.

Axe is the executor, not the scheduler. It is designed to be composed with standard Unix tools — cron, git hooks, pipes, file watchers — rather than reinventing scheduling or workflow orchestration.

## Features

## Review Results: v1.5.0 Release Changes

### 1. Version Bumps — PASS

| File | Old | New | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `cmd/root.go:12` | `1.4.0` | `1.5.0` | Correct |
| `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go:43` | `1.4.0` | `1.5.0` | Correct |
| `cmd/version_test.go:19,24-25` | `1.4.0` | `1.5.0` | Correct (both `TestVersionCommand_Output` and `TestVersionConstant`) |

No remaining `1.4.0` references found in any `.go` file.

### 2. CHANGELOG.md — PASS

- **Section header**: `## [1.5.0] - 2026-03-20` — correct format and date.
- **Keep a Changelog structure**: `### Added` (3 items) and `### Changed` (2 items) — valid categories, properly formatted.
- **Reference link**: `[1.5.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.4.0...v1.5.0` at line 84 — correct base tag (`v1.4.0`) and target (`v1.5.0`).
- **Ordering**: New section appears at the top (before `[1.4.0]`), link appears first in the link list. Correct.

### 3. README.md — PASS (all checks)

**Features list:**
- "Local agent directories" — present at line 28
- "Output allowlist" — present at line 32
- "Token budgets" — present at line 33
- Inserted in a logical position (between existing features, before MCP)

**Run Flags table:**
- `--max-tokens <int>` row at line 271 — correct default (`0 (unlimited)`) and description
- `--agents-dir <path>` row at line 275 — correct default (`(auto-discover)`) and description

**Exit Codes table:**
- Present at lines 277–285 with codes 0–4, all correctly described
- Table has proper header separator (`|---|---|`)

**Agent Configuration example TOML:**
- `allowed_hosts` at line 301 — present with example values
- `[budget]` section at lines 323–324 — present with `max_tokens = 50000`

**Output Allowlist section:**
- Present at lines 358–372
- Behavior table has 5 rows (empty/absent, non-empty, private IPs, redirects, sub-agents) — all with header separator

**Token Budget section:**
- Present at lines 374–393
- Includes TOML example, flag example (`--max-tokens 10000`), precedence rule, exit code 4 behavior, and `--verbose`/`--json` field documentation

**Local Agent Directories section:**
- Present at lines 493–528
- Auto-Discovery subsection with directory tree example (lines 497–506)
- Explicit Override subsection with `--agents-dir` example (lines 508–516)
- Resolution Order subsection with numbered 1-2-3 list (lines 518–524)
- Smart Scaffolding subsection (lines 526–528)

**Content integrity:**
- No existing content removed — the diff is strictly additive (+110 lines, -3 lines for version strings only)
- All markdown tables have proper pipe formatting and header separators (`|---|`)
- No unclosed code blocks — all fenced blocks open and close correctly
- The Providers section, License section, and all pre-existing content remain intact

### Summary

**No issues found.** All five files are consistent, properly formatted, and ready to commit as the v1.5.0 release prep.
